title: daily brief
intro: Two meetings need extra prep this morning: Acme still has a procurement thread open, and Northstar needs a clearer pricing reset before the conversation drifts.

about.title: A daily briefing to prepare you for the meetings ahead.
about.body: Synthesized from your past conversations and background research conducted since you last spoke. Stay present while Granola works quietly in the background.

credit.label: Built for local daily briefing runs

meetings.headline: Roadmap Alignment

person.name: Alice Chen
person.bio: Alice is driving the rollout internally and has been the most consistent advocate for moving quickly, but she gets cautious when procurement and implementation owners are not clearly lined up.

meetings.context: This conversation should close the loop on implementation timing and determine whether procurement has actually cleared the path. The last meeting ended with interest, but not yet enough operational certainty to lock a date.
prepNotes: Start by confirming whether the revised implementation timeline was reviewed internally.
Ask directly whether procurement approved the updated order form or whether another stakeholder entered the process.
Push toward naming the rollout owner and landing a concrete next milestone before the call ends.

meetings.headline: Commercial Reset

person.name: Maya Singh
person.bio: Maya is practical, detail-oriented, and usually receptive when the commercial logic is easy to explain in one pass. She tends to disengage when too many pricing scenarios are introduced at once.

meetings.context: The goal here is to simplify the pricing thread and remove ambiguity from the last call. Northstar did not push back on budget so much as on clarity, which means a cleaner framing could unlock a fast next step.
prepNotes: Reframe annual versus quarterly pricing in plain language before discussing concessions.
Check whether legal surfaced any blockers after the last conversation.
End with a single recommended commercial path rather than presenting three options.
